Package sailpoint.object

Class AbstractCertifiableEntity

  • java.lang.Object
    • sailpoint.tools.xml.AbstractXmlObject
      • sailpoint.object.SailPointObject
        • sailpoint.object.AbstractCertifiableEntity
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.io.Serializable, java.lang.Cloneable, sailpoint.tools.xml.PersistentXmlObject, sailpoint.tools.xml.XMLReferenceTarget
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    AccountGroup, Bundle, DataOwnerCertifiableEntity, Identity, ManagedAttribute
    public abstract class AbstractCertifiableEntity
extends SailPointObject
implements java.lang.Cloneable
    This abstract class includes all of the methods needed for classes that undergo certification. This base class provides the methods that the generic certification classes need, while properties specific to any subclasses are only referenced in the CertificationContext implementation specific to the subclass type.
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Constructor Detail

      • AbstractCertifiableEntity

        public AbstractCertifiableEntity()

    • Method Detail

      • getFullName

        public abstract java.lang.String getFullName()
        Gets the full name of the entity. This is used to construct the names and descriptions of certifications.
        Returns:
        The full name of the entity.

      • getBundles

        public java.util.List<Bundle> getBundles​(java.util.List<Application> apps)
        Gets the list of detected roles on the entity that reference any of the given applications. If the application list is null or empty all roles are returned.
        Parameters:
        apps - Applications by which the role list is filtered (null or empty will return all roles).
        Returns:
        list of detected roles for the given entity.

      • getBundles

        public java.util.List<Bundle> getBundles()
        Returns:
        List of all detected roles for this entity.

      • getBundles

        public java.util.List<Bundle> getBundles​(Application app)
        Gets the detected roles on the entity that reference the given application. If the application is null, all detected roles are returned.
        Parameters:
        app - Applications by which the role list is filtered (null or empty will return all roles).
        Returns:
        list of matching roles

      • getRoleDetections

        public java.util.List<RoleDetection> getRoleDetections()
        Get the role detections on the entity
        Returns:
        List of all role detections

      • getRoleDetections

        public java.util.List<RoleDetection> getRoleDetections​(Application app)
        Get the role detections on the entity that reference the given application. If the application is null, all role detections are returned
        Parameters:
        app - Applications by which the role list is filtered (null or empty will return all roles).
        Returns:
        List of matching DetectedRoles

      • getRoleDetections

        public java.util.Collection<RoleDetection> getRoleDetections​(java.util.List<Application> apps)
        Gets the list of role detections on the entity that reference any of the given applications. If the application list is null or empty all role detections are returned.
        Parameters:
        apps - Applications by which the role list is filtered (null or empty will return all roles).
        Returns:
        Collection of matching RoleDetections for the given entity

      • getAssignedRoles

        public java.util.List<Bundle> getAssignedRoles()
        Gets the assigned roles on the entity
        Returns:
        Distinct List of assigned roles

      • getRoleAssignments

        public java.util.List<RoleAssignment> getRoleAssignments()
        Gets the role assignments on the entity
        Returns:
        List of all role assignments for this entity

      • getExceptions

        public java.util.List<EntitlementGroup> getExceptions​(java.util.List<Application> apps)
        Get the exceptions on this entity that reference the given Applications. If the Applications list is null or empty, all exceptions for this entity are returned.
        Parameters:
        apps - Applications by which the role list is filtered (null or empty will return all roles).
        Returns:
        List of EntitlementGroups assigned to this entity

      • getLatestCertification

        public CertificationLink getLatestCertification​(Certification.Type type)
        Get the latest certification for the given type.
        Parameters:
        type - Certification type
        Returns:
        Latest CertificationLink or null if not found.

      • remove

        public void remove​(CertificationLink link)
        Remove certification link
        Parameters:
        link - CertificationLink to be removed.

      • getTypeName

        public abstract java.lang.String getTypeName​(boolean plural)
        Returns a UI friendly name for the entity type. Currently this is used when the Certificationer needs to return a warning such as 'no users to certify'.
        Parameters:
        plural - Should the type name be plural?
        Returns:
        Entity type name is pluralized if plural flag is true

      • isDifferencable

        public abstract boolean isDifferencable()
        Indicates that you can difference this entity. In some cases, such as ManagedAttribute objects, you cannot. This allows the certification to skip the differencing step.
        Returns:
        true if this entity can be differenced
        See Also:
        Certificationer.addEntity(sailpoint.api.CertificationContext, sailpoint.object.Certification, sailpoint.object.AbstractCertifiableEntity), Certificationer.renderDifferences(sailpoint.api.CertificationContext, sailpoint.object.Certification, sailpoint.object.CertificationEntity, sailpoint.object.AbstractCertifiableEntity)

      • isInactive

        public boolean isInactive()
        Return if this entity is "inactive". If true, the entity will optionally be put in the inactive list on the certification and not require certification.
        Returns:
        if this entity is "inactive". If the entity does not support the notion of "inactive", this returns false.
